description: Create smmway_v1.5.03.py from v1.5.02, remove auto-review-reply feature, add Service Health Auto-Recovery (sales stabilization), bump version, update Telegram UI
steps: Remove config keys 'auto_review_enabled' and 'auto_review_text' from DEFAULT_CFG (around line 388-389)
Remove the call to _maybe_send_review(o) in _maybe_process_review (around line 1469). Remove the comment '# Авто-ответ на отзыв (если включено)' above it too.
Remove the callback handler for 'autoreview_toggle' (line ~3493-3496) that toggles auto_review_enabled
Remove the callback handler for 'autoreview_text_set' (line ~3511-3513) that asks for review text

Update _show_autoreview_menu function (line ~4515) to remove the auto-reply toggle button, auto-reply text button, and the display of auto-reply status. Keep only the bonus-related UI (bonus toggle, bonus %, min stars). Rename it to something like 'Review Bonus' menu since it now only handles bonuses.
Remove the comment on line ~2331 that references _maybe_process_review for review reply timing

Add new config keys to DEFAULT_CFG for Service Health Auto-Recovery: 'service_recovery_enabled': True, 'service_recovery_check_interval_sec': 300, 'service_recovery_min_success_rate': 0.6, 'service_recovery_window_orders': 20, 'service_recovery_cooldown_sec': 1800, 'service_recovery_auto_reenable': True

Add a new background loop function 'service_recovery_loop()' in section 11 (after auto_deactivate_loop). This loop: (a) runs every service_recovery_check_interval_sec, (b) iterates over all active lots, (c) uses DW.get_service_health_score() to check each service's health, (d) if health score < threshold (derived from service_recovery_min_success_rate), marks the service as 'recovering' - sets lot.active=False temporarily via FunPay API and logs it, (e) after cooldown period, re-enables the lot and resets the DW fail streak for that service, (f) sends Telegram notifications on pause and recovery events
Add a recovery tracking dict to the DynamicWorkflows class or as module-level: _recovering_services: dict[int, float] mapping service_id to timestamp when recovery started
Register service_recovery_loop in init_plugin's background loop list (the for fn, name in (...) tuple at line ~4700)

Add a Telegram menu '_show_recovery_menu(c)' that shows: list of currently recovering services, overall service health summary (healthy/warning/critical counts), toggle for service_recovery_enabled, and adjust threshold/cooldown settings
Add a button in the main menu for the recovery feature: a button like '🛡 Стабилизатор' that opens _show_recovery_menu
Add callback handlers for: 'recovery' (open menu), 'recovery_toggle' (enable/disable), 'recovery_threshold_set' (ask for new threshold), 'recovery_cooldown_set' (ask for new cooldown)
Update the plugin DESCRIPTION string to mention the new Service Health Auto-Recovery feature
acceptance_criteria: smmway_v1.5.03.py exists and parses without syntax errors (python -c 'import ast; ast.parse(...)')
No references to 'auto_review_enabled' or 'auto_review_text' in DEFAULT_CFG
No _maybe_send_review function exists in the new file
The call to _maybe_send_review is removed from _maybe_process_review
auto_review_bonus_enabled and _maybe_place_review_bonus remain intact and functional
New config keys for service recovery exist in DEFAULT_CFG
service_recovery_loop function exists and follows the existing background loop pattern
Recovery loop is registered in init_plugin
Telegram menu has a recovery/stabilizer button and corresponding handler
_show_autoreview_menu only shows bonus-related settings (no auto-reply toggle/text)
findings: DW.reset_service_stats already existed in v1.5.02 so no new method was needed. The existing method pops the service from _service_stats entirely rather than resetting to zeroes, which is fine for recovery purposes. All verification checks pass cleanly. Review fixes applied: (1) recovery state persisted to RECOVERY_STATE_FILE, loaded on init_plugin; (2) sliding-window scoring implemented via deque in _recent_results, sized by service_recovery_window_orders; (3) bounds validation added in on_state_message for threshold [0.0,1.0] and cooldown min 60; (4) race guard added in auto_deactivate_loop to skip reactivation for recovering services; (5) get_service_health_score uses windowed success_rate when available.
